title: JCL Security Analysis
description: Analyze JCL for security implications and privilege escalation paths.

steps.title: Job Identity
steps.expected: Identify who the job runs as and when that identity is established.
steps.hint: USER= on JOB card specifies the submitter. Job runs under this identity.
steps.assessment: Q2: Authority is evaluated at job execution, using submitter's identity.

steps.title: Deferred Execution
steps.expected: Understand the time gap between submission and execution.
steps.hint: TYPRUN=HOLD delays execution. The submitter's authority is checked later.
steps.assessment: Q3: Deferred execution - submit now, run later under original identity.

steps.title: Sensitive Dataset Access
steps.expected: Identify data exfiltration via batch job.
steps.hint: JCL can copy any dataset the submitter has READ access to.
steps.assessment: Q3: Batch jobs can access data under submitter's authority silently.

steps.title: RACF Surrogate
steps.expected: Recognize surrogate submission and its authorization.
steps.hint: Submitting as another user requires SURROGAT class permission in RACF.
steps.assessment: Q2: RACF controls who can submit jobs as other users.
